title: CLI Compatibility Expectations
goal: Implement #609 by adding repo-owned CLI compatibility expectations and a descriptive comparison payload against the invoked CLI identity.
non_goals: Do not implement status/doctor warning classes, remediation guidance, or health changes from #610.
Do not mandate uv, npm, global installs, or any single package manager.

intent.outcome: Host repos can declare expected Agentic Workspace CLI posture and see whether the invoked CLI satisfies it.
intent.constraints: Compatibility expectations are repo-owned; workspace.cli_invoke remains local command guidance and is not proof of invocation.
intent.latitude: Choose a conservative config shape that supports version, source class, target relation, and advisory/blocking enforcement.
intent.escalation: Escalate if implementation expands into lifecycle warnings, automatic upgrades, or host dependency mutation.

execution.next_step: Archive this slice and continue the larger lane with #610 when CLI drift reporting is next.
execution.proof: Focused tests and proof-selected commands passed for changed paths.

invariants: repo-owned expectations are advisory/blocking data, not automatic mutation
payload drift remains separate from executable compatibility

active_milestone.scope: Add repo-owned CLI compatibility expectations and comparison output.

immediate_next_action: Archive this completed #609 slice and leave #610 as the next CLI drift slice.
invariants: Compatibility output is descriptive and does not mutate host repos.
Payload drift status remains a separate diagnostic.
contract_decisions_to_freeze: Use a repo-owned [cli_compatibility] table with enforcement, version, source class, and target relation expectations.
open_questions_to_close: Closed for this slice: #609 owns descriptive compatibility comparison; #610 owns warning classes, remediation, and health semantics.

durable_residue.canonical owner now: code and schemas changed in this slice

signals found: summary still promoted the archived #608 continuation even though the roadmap lane already names #609 as next
local external-intent cache still reported #608 open immediately after it was closed upstream
startup compact-size test caught that unconditional cli_compatibility output was too noisy for no-expectation repos
report --section cli_compatibility made the new surface cheap to inspect without reading the full report
finished-work continuation routing did not recognize the roadmap lane refs field and needed an explicit roadmap reason containing the parent issue
signals fixed: start/startup-report now omit cli_compatibility when no expectation is configured, while config/report/status retain the detailed no-expectation payload
signals routed: stale external-intent cache freshness and archived-continuation pressure should be considered in a later planning/router refinement
finished-work routing should read roadmap refs directly instead of requiring issue ids in title/reason/issues
#610 remains the next CLI lane slice for warning/remediation semantics
